content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 26, 1989


Bruce W. Robeck
Paralegal Administrator
Political Reports Unit
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello and Mueller
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-89-580

Dear Mr. Robeck:

     We have received your letter of October 4, 1989, seeking
confirmation of telephone advice provided to you regarding cumula-
tion of contributions made separately by one spouse from community
funds. This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects my
advice that, because of the contribution limitations imposed by
passage of Proposition 73, spouses may now make individual
contributions from community funds. Thus, political contributions
which are made separately by one spouse from community funds are
not cumulated with those of the other spouse for purposes of
qualification as a major donor nor for disclosure on the other
spouse's campaign statement. (See Ishihara Advice Letter, No.
I-89-508, copy enclosed.)¹

     Enclosed for your convenience is a copy of your letter. Do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901 if you have further
questions regarding this matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Blanca M. Breeze
                                            Counsel, Legal Division
KED:BMB:plh
Enclosure

___________________________
¹ On November 7, 1989, the Commission adopted Regulation 18533
(copy enclosed) concerning contributions from joint checking ac-
counts. This regulation provides guidance for allocation of
contributions from contributors who use joint checking accounts.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SAN FRANCISCO

650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2600
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

FILE NUMBER

9999

October 4, 1989

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Attorney, Legal Division
Fair Political Practice Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Telephone Advice

Dear Ms. Breeze:

I am writing to confirm the telephone advice you
provided to me on Friday, September 29, 1989. My understanding
of the question and the conclusion follows.

Question

Are political contributions which are made separately
by a spouse from community funds cumulated for purposes of
qualification as a major donor by the other spouse [Government
Code Section 82013(c)] and for disclosure on the other spouse's
campaign statement?

Conclusion

Political contributions which are made separately by a
spouse from community funds are not cumulated with the other
spouse's political contributions for purposes of qualification as
a major donor nor for disclosure on the other spouse's campaign
statement.

Please correct me if I have misstated either the
question or the conclusion.

Sincerely,

Bruce W. Robeck
Paralegal Administrator
Political Reports Unit
Sacramento

BWR:mjsw

October 11, 1989


Bruce W. Robeck
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-580

Dear Mr. Robeck:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on October 6, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Blanca Breeze an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
